step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the selection of Option A, fecal fat estimation. This test directly quantifies unabsorbed dietary fats in the stool, offering robust evidence of malabsorption particularly involving lipids. It has high sensitivity and specificity compared to less targeted methods such as serum enzyme levels or indirect markers without direct correlation to absorptive capacity. Thus, my final answer remains A: Fecal Fat Estimation. Agreeing fully based on these points reinforces why it stands out among available choices.
